items.name: Fastmail
items.source_story_title: Gmail thinks I'm stupid, so I left
items.summary: Fastmail — privacy-focused email host and Gmail migration target.
items.why_included: Commenters repeatedly named it as the cleanest Gmail escape hatch.
items.evidence: HN commenters said Gmail’s AI nudges pushed them toward Fastmail, including custom-domain migrations.

items.name: mailbox.org
items.source_story_title: Gmail thinks I'm stupid, so I left
items.summary: mailbox.org — secure email and workspace host with strong value.
items.why_included: It was recommended as a practical, lower-cost Gmail alternative.
items.evidence: The thread cited its annual price, aliases, multiple domains, and family accounts as a strong package.

items.name: Proton Mail
items.source_story_title: Gmail thinks I'm stupid, so I left
items.summary: Proton Mail — encrypted email service for privacy-minded users.
items.why_included: It was part of the thread’s main shortlist of Gmail exits.
items.evidence: Commenters listed it alongside Fastmail and mailbox.org, while noting possible deliverability tradeoffs.

items.name: HP-16C Collector's Edition
items.source_story_title: HP re-releases classic computer science calculator: The HP-16C
items.summary: HP-16C Collector's Edition — reissue of HP's programmer calculator.
items.why_included: The thread showed real nostalgia and practical affection for the classic calculator.
items.evidence: Commenters discussed the preorder price, official licensee status, and how original HP calculators still get used.

items.name: SwissMicros DM16C
items.source_story_title: HP re-releases classic computer science calculator: The HP-16C
items.summary: SwissMicros DM16C — modern programmer calculator for HP-16C fans.
items.why_included: It came up as the trusted fallback while waiting for HP’s reissue.
items.evidence: A commenter said they bought a DM16C while waiting for the original re-release.

items.name: SwissMicros DM42n
items.source_story_title: HP re-releases classic computer science calculator: The HP-16C
items.summary: SwissMicros DM42n — modern replacement for the classic HP-42S.
items.why_included: The thread treated it as the stronger SwissMicros recommendation.
items.evidence: Commenters recommended the DM42/DM42n as the modern reimplementation for HP-42S fans.

items.name: NomadNet
items.source_story_title: Age verification for social media, the beginning of the end for a free internet?
items.summary: NomadNet — peer-to-peer networking app for the Reticulum stack.
items.why_included: It stood out as a concrete alternative to the centralized web.
items.evidence: A commenter said they enjoyed playing with NomadNet and Reticulum as a P2P alternative to the WWW.

items.name: GitButler
items.summary: GitButler — Git-backed change manager for parallel agent workflows.
items.why_included: It was singled out as the better worktree-era alternative.
items.evidence: The Copilot App thread explicitly praised GitButler as a stronger option than worktree-like variants.

items.name: LazyWorktree
items.summary: LazyWorktree — TUI for creating and managing git worktrees.
items.why_included: It was mentioned as a beloved workflow tool for isolated repo work.
items.evidence: HN commenters called lazyworktree a favored way to manage worktrees during agent-heavy coding.

items.name: jeep
items.summary: jeep — Janet package manager for vendoring deps and modern installs.
items.why_included: It was recommended as the practical package-management answer in the Janet thread.
items.evidence: A commenter asked “Have you tried jeep?” and said it lets Janet users vendor deps and install modern packages easily.

items.name: Adafruit
items.source_story_title: Adafruit receives demand letter from Fenwick legal counsel on behalf of Flux.ai
items.summary: Adafruit — maker hardware retailer and learning platform.
items.why_included: The dispute thread gave Adafruit unusually strong reputation credit.
items.evidence: Commenters praised its products, documentation, shipping, support, and breakout-board ecosystem.
